rationale: The citing paper uses Ho and Chong (2003) for more than background listing. In the literature review, it identifies that paper as a key extension of attribute-based SKU models that incorporates attribute-level and product-specific experience effects. In the model section, it explicitly states "Following Ho and Chong (2003)" when defining familiarity-based experience variables, indicating direct methodological borrowing for its preference-evolution component.
relation_description: The cited paper is used as a methodological and conceptual foundation for modeling experience/familiarity effects in SKU-level choice. The citing study builds its own topic-based framework on top of that broader attribute-based tradition while reusing Ho and Chong's familiarity operationalization logic.
